name: NonEmpty

unions.name: NonEmpty
unions.comment: A list of a\`s with at least 1 element.

values.name: all
values.comment:
Determine if all elements satisfy some test.

    all (\x -> modBy 2 == 0) (range 1 5) == False

values.name: any
values.comment:
Determine if any elements satisfy some test.

    any (\x -> modBy 2 == 0) (range 1 5) == True

values.name: append
values.comment:
Put two lists together.

    append (range 1 3) (range 4 5) == range 1 5
values.type: NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a

values.name: concat
values.comment: Concatenate a bunch of lists into a single list

values.name: concatMap
values.comment:
Map a given function onto a list and flatten the resulting lists.

    concatMap f xs == concat (map f xs)
values.type: (a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b

values.name: cons
values.comment: Add an element to the front of a list.

values.name: create
values.comment: Create list from first element and tail

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Drop (at most) the first n elements.

The resulting list will have at least one element

    drop 2 (range 1 5)
        |> toList
        == [ 3, 4, 5 ]

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Keep elements that satisfy the test.

Return a list, since result might be empty.

values.name: filterMap
values.comment:
Filter out certain values.

Return a list, since result might be empty.
values.type: (a -> Maybe.Maybe b) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> List.List b

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Reduce a list from the left.
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Reduce a list from the right.
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Try to create sequence from a list with at least two elements

values.name: generate
values.comment:
Generate a new List from iterating over pairs of elements in the NonEmpty list.

    generate Tuple.pair (range 1 5)
        == [ ( 1, 2 ), ( 2, 3 ), ( 3, 4 ), ( 4, 5 ) ]
values.type: (a -> a -> b) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> List.List b

values.name: getAt
values.comment:
Returns Just the element at the given index in the list, or Nothing if the index is out of range.

    getAt 1 (range 1 5) == Just 2

values.name: head
values.comment:
Return the first element

    head (range 1 5) == 1

values.name: indexedMap
values.comment: Same as map but the function is also applied to the index of each element (starting at zero).
values.type: (Basics.Int -> a -> b) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Insert element at given position.

    insertAt 2 42 negate (range 1 5)
        |> toList
        == [ 1, 2, 42, 3, 4, 5 ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a

values.name: intersperse
values.comment: Places the given value between all members of the given list.

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Always return False, since NonEmpty is never empty :)

values.name: length
values.comment:
Determine the length of a list.

    length (range 1 5) == 5

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function to every element of a list.

values.name: map2
values.comment: Apply a two argument function pairwise to elements of both lists
values.type: (a -> b -> r) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty r

values.name: map3
values.comment: Apply a 3 argument function tuples of elements from all lists
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> r) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty c -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty r

values.name: map4
values.comment: Apply a 4 argument function tuples of elements from all lists
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> r) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty c -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty d -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty r

values.name: map5
values.comment: Apply a 5 argument function tuples of elements from all lists
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> r) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty b -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty c -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty d -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty e -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty r

values.name: maximum
values.comment:
Find the maximum element in a non-empty list.

    maximum (range 1 5) == 5

values.name: member
values.comment:
Figure out whether a list contains a value.

    member 42 (range 1 3) == False

values.name: minimum
values.comment:
Find the minimum element in a non-empty list.

    minimum (range 1 5) == 5

values.name: partition
values.comment:
Partition a list based on some test.

The first list contains all values that satisfy the test, and the second list contains all the value that do not.
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> ( List.List a, List.List a )

values.name: product
values.comment: Get the product of the list elements.

values.name: range
values.comment:
Create list from a range of values.

The first value is guaranteed to appear in the list.

values.name: reduce
values.comment:
Reduce a list from left using operator.

This version of foldl does not require an accumulator since the list is guaranteed to not being empty

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Remove the element at an index from a list.

Return the original list if the index is out of range or if the resulting list would be empty.

    removeAt 2 negate (range 1 5)
        |> toList
        == [ 1, 2, 4, 5 ]

values.name: repeat
values.comment: Create a list with n (at least 2) copies of a value

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Reverse a list.

    reverse (range 1 3)
        |> toList
        == [ 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 ]

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a list with only one element

values.name: sort
values.comment: Sort values from lowest to highest

values.name: sortBy
values.comment: Sort values by a derived property.

values.name: sortWith
values.comment:
Sort values with a custom comparison function.

This is also the most general sort function, allowing you to define any other: sort == sortWith compare
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a

values.name: sum
values.comment:
Get the sum of the list elements.

    sum (range 1 5) == 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5

values.name: tail
values.comment:
Extract the rest of the list.

    head (range 1 5) == [ 2, 3, 4, 5 ]

values.name: take
values.comment:
Take (at most) the first n elements.

The number of elements n must be n >= 1, otherwise it returns the singleton list.

    take 2 (range 1 5)
        |> toList
        == [ 1, 2 ]

WARNING: differently from lists, `append (take n lst) (drop n lst)` do not always recover
the original list.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert data to list

values.name: uncons
values.comment:
Deconstruct list into the head and tail parts

    uncons (range 1 5)
        == ( 1, [ 2, 3, 4, 5 ] )

values.name: unzip
values.comment:
Decompose a list of tuples into a tuple of lists.

    unzip (create ( "a", 1 ) [ ( "b", 2 ), ( "c", 3 ) ])
        == ( NonEmpty "a" [ "b", "c" ], NonEmpty 1 [ 2, 3 ] )
values.type: NonEmpty.NonEmpty ( a, b ) -> ( NonEmpty.NonEmpty a, NonEmpty.NonEmpty b )

values.name: updateAt
values.comment:
Update element at the given index

    updateAt 2 negate (range 1 5)
        |> toList
        == [ 1, 2, -3, 4, 5 ]
values.type: Basics.Int -> (a -> a) -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a

values.name: withDefault
values.comment: Create NonEmpty from list, returning a singleton with the given default if list is empty.

values.name: withExample
values.comment: Create NonEmpty from list, returning example if list is empty.
values.type: NonEmpty.NonEmpty a -> List.List a -> NonEmpty.NonEmpty a
